compositional_truth_check.findings: Registry P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not clearly demarcated in the shared black pot. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor.

systemic_losses: P1 (String of Nickels) - No visible specimen in the yellow pot.
new_introductions: Eggshell fragments observed in the P3/P4 pot area, likely used for calcium supplementation or soil aeration.

plant_audit.P1: Empty/Soil only. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Indistinguishable. Appears as a single, struggling stem with minimal foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen remains stable, though growth is stagnant.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, appears to have high organic content. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Significant presence of white eggshell fragments acting as a mulch/amendment.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green leaf in the P3/P4 pot.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: Progressive darkening of the environment; the P3/P4 leaf shows slight chlorosis or loss of turgor pressure. The rabbit anchor remains stationary.

visual_health_inference.P2/P4: High risk of failure; the single leaf is the only indicator of life.

anomalies: Eggshell debris suggests an active, albeit non-traditional, horticultural intervention.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of arrested development. The Pothos (P3) is the most resilient occupant, while the other pots show signs of neglect or failed propagation. The lack of light is the primary limiting factor, evidenced by the lack of new growth and the static nature of the specimens over the 5-day observation period. The eggshells are a notable human intervention, likely intended to buffer soil pH or provide nutrients, but they have not yet stimulated visible growth.
